title: lamyra hispanica
side_effects: Lamyra hispanica can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high saponin content. Ingestion of large amounts can also lead to vomiting, abdominal pain, and in severe cases, kidney damage. The plant's toxic compounds can also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, resulting in adverse reactions. Improper use can also cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues in sensitive individuals.
